commands.review_summary:
This PR adds caller-owned realpath and ancestor-path caches to plugin hook/skill resolution and sandbox host-path checks, with stale symlink and ancestor regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +115. Total +187 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence current-head runtime reproduction was established in this review. Source and dependency inspection show repeated uncached realpath/ancestor checks are plausible, but the linked Windows proof is not for the current head.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer action for current-head real behavior proof and security-boundary review; no narrow automated repair is identified.

target.title: fix(infra): scope Windows path realpath caches
target.body:
## Summary

Windows `lstat`/realpath calls are expensive enough that repeated path-boundary checks can dominate startup and agent-path flows. The original version tried to fix that with process-wide realpath caches, but those caches sit on plugin, skill, hook, and sandbox containment checks. That is too broad: stale symlink or ancestor answers can survive after the filesystem changes.

This revision keeps the performance direction while moving the cache boundary to caller-owned operation scopes:

- `src/infra/path-safety.ts` and `src/infra/path-guards.ts` continue to expose the `@openclaw/fs-safe` caller-cache contract; no process-wide realpath cache is installed.
- `src/infra/boundary-path.ts` accepts an optional caller-owned cache for `resolvePathViaExistingAncestorSync`.
- Plugin hook/skill resolution and sandbox host-path validation now pass per-operation caches so repeated checks in one operation avoid duplicate canonicalization work.
- Regression tests assert stale symlink/junction and ancestor changes are detected without global invalidation, including native Windows via directory junctions.

Closes #85262

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reduces repeated Windows path canonicalization in plugin hook/skill and sandbox host-path operations without adding stale process-wide path answers to security-sensitive containment checks.

Real environment tested: macOS local linked worktree for focused unit coverage; GitHub Actions `windows-2025` native Windows and WSL2 probe; GitHub Actions Windows CI `checks-windows-node-test`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/path-safety.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/hooks/plugin-hooks.test.ts src/skills/loading/plugin-skills.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/host-paths.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/path-safety.ts src/infra/path-guards.ts src/infra/boundary-path.ts src/infra/path-safety.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/hooks/plugin-hooks.ts src/skills/loading/plugin-skills.ts src/agents/sandbox/host-paths.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Remote proof:

- Windows/WSL probe: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26691338015
  - native Windows: `Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26100.0`, `arch=X64`
  - WSL2: imported `UbuntuProbe`, `Linux ... microsoft-standard-WSL2`, `PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 24.04 LTS"`, `wsl2_ok=true`
- CI Windows node lane: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26691338037/job/78668145318
- CI infra process lane: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26691338037/job/78668145571

Evidence after fix:

- Focused Vitest: 5 shards passed, 114 tests passed.
- Direct oxlint on touched files passed.
- Autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Native Windows CI node-test passed.
- Native Windows + WSL2 probe passed, including WSL2 import and execution.
- Stale symlink/junction tests now run on Windows instead of skipping.

Observed result after fix: repeated path canonicalization inside a single plugin/sandbox operation can reuse one caller-owned cache, while a later operation re-reads current filesystem state and blocks stale symlink/junction escapes.

What was not tested: a new full 120s Windows CPU profile for the scoped-cache version. The original issue profile established the `lstat` bottleneck, but the scoped version intentionally trades some of the global-cache speedup for correct cache lifetime and should be profiled before treating the exact 83% lstat reduction as reproduced.

## Known unrelated CI state

Manual broad CI on this ref currently has unrelated failures in `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts`:

```text
src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts(844,32): error TS2345: Argument of type 'number | undefined' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts(891,28): error TS2345: Argument of type 'number | undefined' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
```

The same failure reproduced in the remote `pnpm check:changed` gate and in CI `check-lint` / `check-prod-types`; this PR does not touch that file.
